resources.string: Unlock to access your local identity and paired contacts.
The Vault could not be unlocked. Check the device lock and try again.
The Android Keystore key is no longer valid. Existing protected data cannot be opened; pairing must be set up again after a safe reset.

The old identity, contacts, sessions, pending messages, and replay records will be permanently deleted. Contacts on other devices will not trust the new identity. Pair with every contact again and compare the new Safety Number.
The inaccessible Vault could not be fully reset. No new identity was created. Restart CipherBoard and try again.
Set a secure device PIN or password before creating the Vault.
CipherBoard creates the cryptographic identity on this device. No account, phone number, email, or network connection is used. Clearing app data changes this identity and requires pairing again.
This name stays on this device and is not part of your cryptographic identity.
The local identity could not be created. Check the name and try again.
Protected local data could not be read safely.
Contact pairing is not available right now.
No paired contacts yet. Add a contact and complete the two-way QR scan in person.

CipherBoard is an unofficial modified HeliBoard distribution. These documents are available offline. The exact corresponding source is distributed as a versioned source archive alongside the release APK; BUILD_INFO identifies its Git commit.
This bundled license document could not be read.
CipherBoard reduces specific risks by encrypting messages before they reach an SMS, messenger, or email app. It does not eliminate every way a message or device can be compromised.
Sender identity is trusted only after both people compare the Safety Number during the in-person QR pairing and mark the contact as verified.
What CipherBoard is designed to protect
Carriers, messenger services, email providers, and compromised transport accounts receive ciphertext instead of message text.
Authenticated encryption detects changed or forged messages. Persisted ratchet state rejects replay and supports bounded out-of-order delivery.
Identity and session records are encrypted under a user-authenticated, non-exportable Android Keystore key. StrongBox is attempted first, with a TEE fallback.

Secure composer text is not committed to the external app, clipboard, keyboard learning, logs, notifications, or plaintext history. Protected message windows block normal screenshots and recent-app previews.
The runtime has no Internet permission, uses no server or cloud account, and excludes protected data from Android backup and device transfer.
What CipherBoard cannot fully protect against
An attacker controlling an unlocked device or reading its live screen.
Root access, kernel or operating-system exploits, compromised firmware, or a faulty Keystore or StrongBox implementation.
A malicious or substituted CipherBoard APK, a compromised build toolchain, or a stolen signing key.
A camera recording the screen or another person reading over your shoulder. Screenshot blocking is not an absolute screen-capture guarantee.
Malicious Accessibility, screen-reader, input, device-admin, or similarly privileged services.
Traffic analysis and metadata exposed to the transport, including who communicated, when, and approximate message size.
Physical coercion or disclosure of the device PIN, password, or biometric access.
Defects in the cryptographic library, Android integration, parser, storage, pairing flow, or user interface.
CipherBoard uses established cryptographic primitives, but the complete product has not undergone an independent security audit.

Before high-risk use, verify the APK signing certificate and SHA-256 from a trusted source, keep verified boot and the bootloader locked, use a strong device PIN or password, disable untrusted Accessibility services, compare every Safety Number in person, and obtain an independent applied-cryptography and Android security review.
